data.title: Brazilian President Lula Blocks U.S. Official's Visit
data.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has taken steps to prevent a U.S. official from visiting Brazil. According to Jin10, this decision marks a significant diplomatic move by the Brazilian government. The reasons behind the restriction have not been disclosed, but it reflects ongoing tensions between Brazil and the United States. This development could impact bilateral relations and future diplomatic engagements between the two nations.

data.title: Latin American Nations Call for Halt to Hostile Actions by Iran
data.description: Mexico, Colombia, and Brazil have issued a joint statement urging Iran to cease its hostile actions. According to Jin10, the three Latin American countries expressed their concerns over the escalating tensions involving Iran and called for a peaceful resolution to the ongoing issues. The statement highlights the importance of diplomatic efforts in maintaining regional stability and preventing further conflict. The countries emphasized their commitment to international peace and security, urging all parties involved to engage in constructive dialogue.

data.title: Amazon to Integrate Cerebras Systems Chips with Trainium for Enhanced AI Software Performance
data.description: Amazon has announced its intention to incorporate chips from Cerebras Systems, a startup, alongside its proprietary Trainium processors. Bloomberg posted on X that this strategic move aims to enhance the performance of AI software. The collaboration between Amazon and Cerebras Systems is expected to optimize the processing capabilities required for advanced AI applications. By leveraging the strengths of both chip technologies, Amazon seeks to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of its AI operations.

data.title: Vivid Seats Faces Financial Struggles Amid Earnings Miss
data.description: Vivid Seats's debt has fallen deeper into distressed levels following the company's earnings report, which failed to meet expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that the ticket reseller also projected weaker financial performance for the remainder of the year. The disappointing earnings and guidance have raised concerns about the company's financial stability.

data.title: Canada's Economy Faces Major Adjustments Due to U.S. Trade Actions
data.description: Canadian Prime Minister Carney has highlighted significant economic adjustments in Canada as a result of recent U.S. trade measures. According to Jin10, these trade actions have prompted shifts in Canada's economic landscape, necessitating strategic responses from policymakers. The adjustments underscore the interconnected nature of the North American economies and the impact of international trade policies on domestic economic conditions.

data.title: India to Cancel IDBI Bank Stake Sale Due to Low Bids
data.description: India plans to cancel the bids received for a majority stake in IDBI Bank as they fell short of the minimum price required, according to sources. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the decision comes after evaluating the financial offers submitted by potential buyers. The government had aimed to divest its stake in the bank to raise funds and reduce its involvement in the banking sector. However, the bids did not meet the financial expectations set by the authorities, leading to the decision to scrap the sale process. The move reflects the challenges faced in attracting investors willing to meet the valuation criteria for state-owned assets. The government will now reassess its strategy for the bank's divestment, considering alternative approaches to achieve its financial objectives.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Launches 'Real Commitment-4' Operation
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard announced the commencement of the 45th wave of the 'Real Commitment-4' operation on Friday evening. According to Jin10, this action follows a significant response from the Iranian people to the Supreme Leader and their participation in the International Quds Day march. The operation underscores the ongoing political and social dynamics within the country, reflecting the government's engagement with its citizens and international events.

data.title: Saudi Arabia Seeks Alternatives to Strait of Hormuz Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Saudi Arabia is experiencing a significant accumulation of oil supertankers off its Red Sea coast, as the kingdom endeavors to circumvent the Strait of Hormuz due to disruptions from the ongoing Iran conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Riyadh's efforts to manage the unprecedented challenges posed by the regional tensions. The strategic move aims to ensure the continued flow of oil exports, as the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments. The buildup of tankers reflects Saudi Arabia's proactive measures to maintain stability in its oil trade amidst geopolitical uncertainties.

data.title: Social Media Platform X Submits Rectification Plan for Verification System
data.description: European Commission spokesperson Thomas Renier announced that social media platform X has submitted a rectification plan for its blue check verification system. According to NS3.AI, Renier made this statement during a press conference on the 13th.

data.title: Analysts Debate STRC Preferred Stock Amid Dividend Concerns
data.description: Analysts are currently discussing the similarities between Strategy's STRC preferred stock and the collapse of Terra's UST. According to NS3.AI, the annualized dividend for STRC has increased to 11.5% on a $100 par value, raising concerns among investors. While STRC does not have the algorithmic death spiral associated with UST, holders of STRC are still exposed to risks related to dividends and the Bitcoin-linked balance sheet.

data.title: Spot silver fell back below $81
data.description: Gate data shows that spot silver fell in the short term, dropping below $81/oz, down 3.46% on the day. New York silver futures fell sharply by 5.00% on the day, currently trading at $80.85/oz.

data.title: US media: US military deploys Marine Corps expeditionary force to the Middle East
data.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, two U.S. officials said the Pentagon is deploying a Marine Expeditionary Force to the Middle East as Iran intensifies its attacks on the Strait of Hormuz. Officials said Defense Secretary Hergesse has approved a request from U.S. Central Command to deploy the expeditionary force, which typically consists of up to 2,500 Marines. This move comes at a time when Iranian attacks on the strait have nearly paralyzed shipping on this strategic waterway, impacting the global economy, driving up gasoline prices, and posing significant military and political challenges for Trump. A Pentagon spokesperson declined to comment.

data.title: Leo Pharma Considers IPO as It Moves Toward Public Listing
data.description: Leo Pharma is reportedly preparing for a potential initial public offering (IPO), according to sources familiar with the matter. Bloomberg posted on X that the Danish pharmaceutical company, which has been in operation for over a century, is engaging investment bankers to compete for roles in the anticipated public listing. The move marks a significant step for Leo Pharma as it explores options to expand its market presence and financial capabilities. The company, known for its dermatology products, is considering this strategic shift amid evolving industry dynamics and market opportunities. While specific details regarding the timing and size of the IPO remain undisclosed, the decision underscores Leo Pharma's ambition to enhance its growth trajectory and leverage public market resources. The potential IPO could provide the company with increased capital to support its research and development initiatives and expand its global footprint. Leo Pharma's exploration of a public listing reflects broader trends in the pharmaceutical sector, where companies are increasingly seeking public investment to fuel innovation and competitiveness. As the company moves forward with its plans, stakeholders and industry observers will be closely monitoring developments related to its IPO strategy.

data.title: AI Companies Under Investigation by U.S. Senators Over Data Center Concerns
data.description: U.S. Democratic senators have initiated an investigation into eight artificial intelligence companies regarding their data center practices. According to Jin10, the inquiry focuses on how these companies manage and utilize data centers, which are crucial for AI operations. The senators aim to ensure compliance with regulations and assess potential risks associated with data handling and privacy. This move reflects growing concerns about the transparency and security of AI technologies. The investigation could lead to increased scrutiny and regulatory measures in the AI sector.

data.title: Grayscale Highlights Solana's Potential for Growth
data.description: Grayscale has expressed optimism about Solana's future, citing several key factors. Grayscale posted on X. The company highlighted Solana's leadership in users, transactions, and fees as a significant advantage. Additionally, Solana is well-positioned for growth due to increasing regulatory clarity. The network also offers staking rewards for participation, which is an attractive feature for users. Despite being approximately 67% below its September 2025 highs, Solana benefits from strong network effects and a diverse on-chain economy, including decentralized finance (DeFi) and decentralized infrastructure (DePin).

data.title: Venture Global Advances $8.6 Billion LNG Expansion in Louisiana
data.description: Venture Global announced plans to proceed with an $8.6 billion expansion of its third liquefied natural gas (LNG) export project in Louisiana. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's commitment to enhancing its LNG infrastructure in the region. This expansion is expected to significantly increase the export capacity, contributing to the growing demand for natural gas globally. The project underscores Venture Global's strategic focus on strengthening its position in the energy sector, particularly in the LNG market. The company aims to leverage this development to meet international energy needs and support economic growth in Louisiana. The expansion is part of a broader initiative to boost LNG exports from the United States, aligning with national energy goals and market trends.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Milestone and Ethereum ETF Developments Highlighted in Weekly Roundup
data.description:
Bitcoin has reached a significant milestone with 20 million coins mined, according to NS3.AI. This achievement marks a notable point in the cryptocurrency's lifecycle, as it approaches its maximum supply limit of 21 million coins. The weekly roundup also highlighted developments in the Ethereum market, with BlackRock's staked Ethereum ETF gaining attention. This financial product is designed to offer investors exposure to Ethereum's staking rewards, reflecting growing interest in Ethereum-based investment vehicles.

Additionally, the report noted a rise in the total value locked (TVL) of tokenized stocks, which has surpassed $1 billion. This increase indicates a growing trend towards the tokenization of traditional assets, allowing for more accessible and efficient trading on blockchain platforms. The developments in both Bitcoin and Ethereum markets underscore the evolving landscape of cryptocurrency investments and the increasing integration of blockchain technology in traditional finance.

data.title: Rabobank: Middle East conflict may continue to support the US dollar
data.description: In a report, Jane Foley of Rabobank stated that the US dollar may still have room to strengthen as the Middle East conflict shows no signs of abating. The dollar's rise since the start of the conflict should put an end to previous debates about whether it had lost its safe-haven appeal after its decline in April last year due to tariffs. She noted that the dollar still faces uncertainty, including how the Federal Reserve will balance political pressure to cut interest rates with inflationary pressures from rising energy prices. However, as an oil-exporting nation, the US's terms of trade may improve as a result. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation releases the "EF Mandate," reaffirming the network's core mission.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation Council released a document titled "EF Mandate," which systematically clarifies its role and responsibilities within the Ethereum ecosystem. The document emphasizes that Ethereum must remain censorship-resistant, open-source, and focused on privacy and security (CROPS) to serve user sovereignty, reduce the risk of economic exploitation, and optimize the user experience. EF states that it will focus on undertaking key tasks that other stakeholders cannot easily handle in the long term, and that this "Mandate" serves as both an internal action plan for its members and a "part charter, part manifesto, and part guideline" for the community to reference.

data.title: Trump: The economy will rebound immediately once the war with Iran ends.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports: US President Trump: The economy will rebound immediately once the war with Iran ends. (Jinshi)
